title: Non-reentrant POSIX functions should be replaced with their reentrant versions
description: A function is called reentrant if it can be interrupted in the middle of its execution and then safely called again ("re-entered") before its previous invocations complete execution. It is especially important that multi-threaded applications do not call the same non-reentrant function from different threads. This rule will trigger an issue each time a function in the configurable list is invoked.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Non-reentrant POSIX function detected. Replace with reentrant alternative (e.g., localtime_r, strtok_r, etc.)
Non-reentrant POSIX function detected. Replace with reentrant alternative (e.g., localtime_r, strtok_r, etc.)
